DGP fit + LGBM+DGP residual EDA

Generated 2026-04-21 06:16:54 by scripts/eda_dgp_residuals.py. Inputs: data/train.csv, scripts/artifacts/oof_lgbm_dgp.npy, scripts/artifacts/bench_dgp_results.json.

1. DGP rule fit on synthetic train

The closed-form rule (scripts/dgp_formula.py) gets raw acc 0.98364, bal_acc 0.96097 on the 630k synthetic training rows. There are 10,304 flipped rows (1.64%). Flips cluster at the score boundaries (3-4 and 6-7), confirming the synthetic = rule + near-threshold label noise.

Left: label counts per DGP score. Right: flip-rate per score; horizontal line = mean flip rate.

2. LGBM+DGP OOF performance

Argmax raw = 0.98570 / bal = 0.96349. Tuned log-bias raw = 0.98177 / bal = 0.97271. Tuning pulls High recall up at the cost of a small drop in the two majority classes — classic balanced-accuracy optimisation.

Confusion matrix — argmax (rows=true, cols=pred)

argmax_recall tuned_recall delta

Medium 0.9757 0.9623 -0.0134
High 0.9188 0.9603 0.0415

3. Residual deep dive (LGBM+DGP tuned)

Of the 11,486 LGBM errors: 3,151 are on rule-correct rows (the model broke a correct rule prediction), and 8,335 are on rule-flipped rows (the model failed to recover a flip). The DGP rule's own error count is 10,304 — so LGBM recovered 1,969 flips and introduced 3,151 new errors it wouldn't have had if it just followed the rule.

Joint: rule-agreement × LGBM-correctness (raw counts)

lgbm lgbm correct lgbm wrong
rule
rule correct 616545 3151
rule flipped 1969 8335

Same, as rates

lgbm lgbm correct lgbm wrong
rule
rule correct 0.9786 0.0050
rule flipped 0.0031 0.0132
Distance-to-threshold densities, errors vs correct. Dashed line = the threshold.
Error mass concentrates at boundary scores (3, 4, 6, 7) — same as the rule flip-rate.
Errors pile up along the rule's decision boundaries (dashed lines).

4. 128-cell lookup table

Each cell = fixed values of the 6 DGP-discrete features (dry, norain, hot, windy, nomulch, stage-active). In the 10k original dataset every cell was pure; in the 630k synthetic training set, 62 of 64 cells have mixed labels — that's where the flip noise lives. 1 cells have a majority label different from the rule's prediction (these are the cells a per-cell-majority predictor would re-label).

Cell purity distribution (left), purity vs cell size colored by score (right).

Top-20 (by n) cells where rule != majority — 1 total such cells
